Framer AI Agents

What do you think? … [...] Roopesh Donde

Loved the aesthetics. I like how instantly it makes me focussed and think on what to write.   
Very minimalist and no distractions.   
One thing I would suggest to make tying sound off on default. Its Novel to hear the sounds at first, but it quickly becomes a distraction and doubles up with the actual keyboard sounds.

Tina Chhabra

there's something nice about a writing tool that just opens and works. no login, no sync setup, no onboarding flow. the ambient writing modes are a cool touch too. would be great to see some kind of simple export to markdown file so you can move notes into other tools when you need to.

Patrick Krekelberg [...] Patrick Krekelberg

Local-first writing is still underrated for unfinished thinking. Draft notes, specs, prompts, and private business planning often need to exist before they are polished enough for a cloud workspace; offline ownership changes the way people are willing to write honestly.

Etienne Garcia

results.title: LumiChats Offline(free): Your AI, fully offline with Zero data collection & 100% free | Product Hunt
results.content:
Following along. Local-first translation is a space that needs more builders.

Aditya Kumar Jha
LumiChats Offline(free)

LumiChats Offline(free)

Really valuable insight especially on register collapse in smaller models. You’re absolutely right that multilingual quality is about more than translation, it’s also tone and context. The conversational-pair fine-tuning point is super helpful too. Really appreciate this thoughtful feedback 🙌

Thami Benjelloun
Mailwarm

Mailwarm

Congrats on the launch. Clear direction: private, offline AI that actually works.

Hard to execute well. Curious how far you push it.

Aditya Kumar Jha
LumiChats Offline(free)

LumiChats Offline(free) [...] Fenly sounds really interesting too 👀 Local first translation is a space that deserves way more attention right now.

Prokopiy

@aditya\_kumar\_jha1 Multilingual fine-tunes at smaller scale is genuinely hard work. Most labs default to English-primary because the multilingual training data is messier and quality is harder to evaluate per-language. Glad you're pushing on it.

One pattern from Fenly's translation side that might be useful: the smaller the model, the more registers (formal/casual/technical) collapse to a single voice. Quality at 4B feels OK on a single text, but feels generic when you hit different contexts back-to-back. The thing that holds best at smaller sizes is targeted fine-tuning on conversational pairs rather than broad multilingual coverage. [...] how does LumiChats performs on normal consumer hardware without a GPU. Which use cases feel strongest today, writing, summarization, coding, document Q&A, vision, or voice and where do you still see the biggest gap versus cloud models?

Germán Merlo
Build Check

Build Check

It's super cool Aditya! Wish you all the best on this impressive launch

Aditya Kumar Jha
LumiChats Offline(free)

LumiChats Offline(free)

@german\_merlo1 Thank you so much Germán 🙌 Really appreciate the support and kind words.

We’re still very early, but we’re building this with a big vision around private, accessible, fully local AI for everyone. A lot more exciting updates are coming soon

Cam Pritchard

The fully offline part is honestly super cool. Feels way more private than most AI tools out there. Congrats!

results.title: Obsidian Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Based on 165 reviews

Review Obsidian?

Reviewers see Obsidian as a fast, local-first Markdown workspace for building a personal knowledge base, with links, backlinks, and graph view repeatedly praised for helping ideas connect and resurface. Users also highlight strong search, cross-device use, and a large plugin community that lets the app fit writing, research, study, and project work. Makers of NextPhone and Novi Notes likewise praise its local-first design. The main caveat: setup, Markdown, and endless customization can feel overwhelming or distracting.

Summarized with AI

Most Informative

Subscribe

community plugins(43)

local storage(30)

customization(29)

markdown support(23)

second brain(22)

graph view(16)

privacy(14)

cross-platform(12) [...] Report

 28 views

•3 reviews

Obsidian is hands down the most capable note-taking tool available today. Its local-first approach ensures privacy and full ownership of your data, while its Markdown foundation keeps things fast, portable, and future-proof. What truly sets Obsidian apart is its extensibility. With a rich plugin ecosystem and support for custom scripts, it transforms into whatever workflow you need—be it task management, knowledge organization, or research tracking. The graph view offers a clear, visual representation of your ideas and how they connect, making it ideal for deep thinkers and professionals alike. Simply put, Obsidian isn't just a note app—it's an environment for serious knowledge work.
